body_txt: When your setting features snarling, ferocious beasts with names like Fluffy the Terrible , call in Alice the Fluffy Tamer. She'll turn that man-eating, three-headed dog into a precious puppy by sundown, and the hardest part of the job will be finding a large enough rawhide bone... But Alice isn't merely good with savage animals, she's the one who names them "Fluffy" in the first place. To her, such creatures are as adorable as any kitten or similar house pet. She would either be mortally offended by the idea of What Measure Is a Non-Cute? or hold it in completely opposite regard than your average person. The main drawback of such a pastime seems to be having to deal with consequences of Muggles and Idiot Heroes freaking out when they see her with her latest pet and mistake her for a Damsel in Distress or something. Repeatedly . Alice is frequently a Friend to All Living Things and/or Animal Lover . Other times this preference may only extend so far and won't encompass all scary beasts but perhaps just a particular kind. She may also be a Nightmare Fetishist , who finds beauty in all things, especially when they're terrifying. A sure sign of a Fluffy Tamer is that she talks to rampaging beasts in Baby Talk (and doesn't get eaten). She can even get close enough to pet them. Alice always wins over the monsters with love and kindness; if she gets her way by frightening them into submission, that's And Call Him "George" . Compare Admiring the Abomination , where the character might not want to tame Fluffy but is still impressed at the synchronization between his three heads; contrast Animals Hate Him , who gets on the bad side of even the most sweet and gentle critters. See also Nightmare Fetishist , The Beastmaster , Domesticated Dinosaurs , Training the Pet . Has nothing to do with a guy named Fluffy . Dragon Tamer is the subtrope where Fluffy is... y'know, a dragon.
